title: Cedarwood and sandalwood beard oil
subtitle: A conditioning oil for beard and skin beneath
excerpt: A lightweight, woody-scented beard oil built on jojoba, sweet almond, and argan oils, scented with cedarwood and sandalwood essential oils. Conditions both the beard hair and the skin underneath. Makes around 30 ml.
sourceNotes: Jojoba oil: technically a wax ester, closely matching human sebum. Sweet almond oil: high oleic acid, lightweight, compatible with facial skin. Argan oil: oleic and linoleic acid balance, rapid absorption. Essential oil safety for facial skin application: Tisserand and Young, Essential Oil Safety (2nd ed., 2014). Cedarwood and sandalwood both at lower than 2% recommended for facial skin use.

recipe.yieldDescription: 1 dropper bottle (~30 ml, approximately 30 to 60 applications)
recipe.batchNotes: Scale proportionally. Keep the combined essential oil content at 2% or below of total formula weight.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: Store in the dropper bottle in a cool dark place. Jojoba is among the most stable of cosmetic oils and the blend keeps 12 months. Discard if an off smell develops.

glossaryTerms.definition: A preparation containing no water phase. This beard oil keeps 12 months without a preservative.

Apply a few drops to the inside of the forearm and leave for 24 to 48 hours before applying to the face. Cedarwood and sandalwood essential oils are generally well-tolerated but individual sensitivity varies.

content.text: A beard oil serves two purposes: conditioning the beard hair and moisturising the skin underneath. Coarse facial hair benefits from oils that penetrate the hair shaft and reduce brittleness; the skin beneath needs a non-comedogenic, lightweight moisturiser. Jojoba is the primary carrier here because it matches the structure of sebum more closely than any other oil, making it compatible with both skin and hair. Sweet almond oil adds oleic acid and a slightly richer feel. Argan oil provides a smooth, fast-absorbing finish. Cedarwood and sandalwood essential oils are both woody base notes that hold well in an
formula; the combined fragrance load is under 2% of formula weight, within the facial-safe range.
Measure each ingredient directly into the amber dropper bottle. Fit the dropper top and roll gently to combine. Label with the date. Ready to use immediately.
on the inside of the forearm for 24 to 48 hours before facial use.

Apply 3 to 5 drops into the palm, rub the palms together, and work through the beard from root to tip. Use fingertips to massage the oil through to the skin underneath. Best applied after washing the face or beard when the hair is still slightly damp, which helps the oil distribute evenly. A small amount goes a long way; too much leaves the beard looking greasy.

Store in the amber dropper bottle in a cool dark place. The blend keeps 12 months. Jojoba is one of the most stable cosmetic oils; the main stability risk in this formula is the argan oil, which can oxidise if stored warm. Discard if an off or sharp smell develops.
